location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel for onboarding, team meetings, and industry events

level_signals: Owns full platform vision and roadmap for segmentation

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or technically complex systems.
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or technically complex systems

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design, performance, and scalability.
Experience working with large-scale data systems, query systems, or real-time/near-real-time evaluation systems.
Track record of building customer-facing products on top of complex backend systems.
Strong analytical skills with experience defining and managing system-level metrics and SLAs.
Ability to balance infrastructure investments with user-facing product improvements.
Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, product, and business stakeholders.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience in marketing technology, personalization systems, or audience platforms is a strong plus.

scope.surface: Klaviyo segmentation platform — lists, segments, audience definition, evaluation, and insights systems
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, marketing automation / CDP for ecommerce
primary_metrics_inferred: Customer ROI / retention improvements driven by segmentation
core_responsibilities: Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo's segmentation platform.
Build and operate segmentation as a first-class data product, optimized for correctness, performance, and scale.
Partner with engineering to improve system performance, including segmentation latency, reliability, and SLA adherence.
Evolve the expressiveness of segmentation (e.g., nested logic, advanced conditions) to meet the needs of mid-market and enterprise customers.
Ensure segment definitions are consistent, explainable, and resilient as data complexity grows.
Develop capabilities that help customers analyze, iterate on, and optimize high-value audiences.
Introduce recommendations and insights to guide customers toward effective segmentation strategies based on best practices and their own data.
Define and track key metrics, including latency (P95), success rate, freshness and correctness SLAs, and system reliability.
Lead cross-functional initiatives across product, engineering, data, and go-to-market teams to improve how audiences are defined and activated.
Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across stakeholders.

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership (segmentation platform with specific metric types named)
Explicit encouragement for close-but-not-exact-match candidates to apply
Role described as strategically critical to Klaviyo's core value proposition
Specific technical depth expected (P95 latency, SLA adherence) signals mature engineering culture
red_flags: No specific cities listed — location type and remote eligibility not fully explicit in JD body; inferred as hybrid/remote-US from travel language
Very broad comp range ($168K–$252K, 50% spread) makes it hard to assess level fit from comp alone
7–10+ years minimum is a hard floor that eliminates most mid-level PMs

fields_inferred_not_stated: location.type (inferred as hybrid from '10% travel' language; no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite statement in JD body)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred from 'cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets' and no city requirement stated)
seniority.level (inferred from title 'Lead PM', years range 7-10+, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and company description)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities section, not stated as goals)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'cost of labor across various U.S. geographic markets' language)
ambiguities: JD does not specify remote vs. onsite vs. hybrid explicitly; the only location signal is '10% travel' requirement and mention of geographic pay variation across US locations. Treated as hybrid/remote-US eligible.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
